title: Seoul evacuated by UN Command Forces
body: United Nations forces were forced to evacuate Seoul after a successful southward advance by Chinese and North Korean forces.

title: Japanese air attacks begin against Rabaul
body: Japanese air attacks began against Rabaul, a major harbour and airfield complex with strategic importance in the south-west Pacific.

title: Bardia captured
body: The Australian 6th Division captured Bardia in Australia’s first major land battle of the Second World War.

title: HMAS Australia damaged by kamikaze aircraft
body: HMAS Australia was struck by kamikaze aircraft while operating in support of Allied landings in the Philippines.

title: HMAS Tobruk bombards Chomi Do
body: HMAS Tobruk bombarded Chomi Do during operations in the Korean War.

title: Evacuation of Helles
body: The evacuation of Helles marked the final withdrawal from the Gallipoli campaign.

title: RAAF launches first Meteor rocket attack
body: RAAF Meteors carried out their first rocket attack against ground forces in Korea.

title: Last Australian servicemen return from Korea
body: The last Australian servicemen returned from Korea after the Korean War armistice period.

title: Last Australian forces sail for home from the Middle East
body: Australia began concentrating the bulk of its forces against Japan after fighting in North Africa and the Middle East.

title: First Japanese air attack on Port Moresby
body: The first Japanese air attack on Port Moresby began the struggle over a key base in New Guinea.

title: Baria recaptured
body: 3rd Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment, recaptured Baria after the Tet Offensive in South Vietnam.

title: Turkish forces attack the Suez Canal
body: Turkish attempts to capture the Suez Canal were repulsed, with Australians later helping garrison the trenches.

title: Australians counter-attack at Wau
body: Australian forces counter-attacked at Wau and eventually forced the Japanese into retreat.

title: Yalta Conference
body: The Yalta Conference shaped post-war Europe as Allied victory in Europe approached.

title: Captain H.W. Murray wins the Victoria Cross
body: Captain H.W. Murray earned the Victoria Cross at Stormy Trench in France.

title: 6th Division enters Benghazi
body: Australian troops entered Benghazi during the North African campaign.

title: State naval and military forces transferred to Commonwealth control
body: Federation began the process of building federal military forces from state forces.

title: HMAS Perth sunk in Sunda Strait
body: HMAS Perth was sunk in battle off Java against overwhelming Japanese forces.

title: Battle of the Bismarck Sea begins
body: Allied air attacks devastated a Japanese convoy bound for Lae during the Battle of the Bismarck Sea.

title: Sudan contingent departed Sydney
body: New South Wales sent a contingent to the Sudan in a demonstration of imperial support.

title: Broome and Wyndham bombed
body: Japanese air raids struck Broome and Wyndham during the Pacific war.

title: HMAS Yarra sunk south of Java
body: HMAS Yarra was lost while escorting a convoy during the fighting in the Netherlands East Indies.

title: HMAS Sydney arrives at Fremantle carrying 5RAR
body: HMAS Sydney returned carrying 5th Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment, from Vietnam.

title: Japanese occupation of Java complete
body: Japanese forces completed the occupation of Java in the Netherlands East Indies campaign.

title: First AIF disbanded
body: The First Australian Imperial Force was formally disbanded after the First World War.

title: Private J.C. Jensen wins the Victoria Cross
body: Private J.C. Jensen won the Victoria Cross at Noreuil, France.

title: Battle of Tamai
body: Tamai was the largest and most significant engagement involving the New South Wales Sudan contingent.

title: First action at Villers-Bretonneux
body: Australian troops helped repel German attacks at Villers-Bretonneux in the final year of the First World War.

title: 3RAR involved in Operation Rugged
body: 3rd Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment, took part in Operation Rugged in Korea.

title: US 41st Division arrives in Australia
body: American forces began arriving in large numbers in Australia during the Pacific war.

title: 1RAR arrives in Korea
body: 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment, arrived in Korea for service in the Korean War.

title: Australians reach the Western Front
body: The first Australian units of the 2nd Division arrived on the Western Front in France.

title: Australian troops invade Tarakan
body: The 26th Brigade of the 9th Division landed on Tarakan in Operation Oboe 1.

title: Soviets take Berlin
body: The fall of Berlin signalled the end of the Second World War in Europe.

title: Second battle of Bullecourt begins
body: Australian divisions fought heavily at Bullecourt during operations against the Hindenburg Line.

title: Australian attack on Gaba Tepe
body: Australian troops attacked Gaba Tepe from the Anzac area in an unsuccessful effort to deny Ottoman observation positions.

title: Battle of the Coral Sea begins
body: The Battle of the Coral Sea began as the first naval engagement fought by aircraft alone.

title: Australians and New Zealanders at Cape Helles
body: The 2nd Australian Brigade and New Zealand Brigade were redeployed from Anzac Cove to Cape Helles.

title: Corporal G.J. Howell performs VC action
body: Corporal G.J. Howell performed the action near Bullecourt for which he was later awarded the Victoria Cross.

title: Warrant Officer R.S. Simpson performs VC action
body: Warrant Officer Class 2 R.S. Simpson performed one of the actions in Vietnam for which he received the Victoria Cross.

title: RAN aircraft first used in combat
body: Royal Australian Navy aircraft were first used in combat from HMAS Sydney and Melbourne.

title: Evacuation from Crete completed
body: The evacuation from Crete was completed after four nights of difficult withdrawal operations.

title: 2RAR arrives in Vietnam
body: 2nd Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment, arrived in Vietnam as Australia’s commitment expanded.

title: Battle of Midway begins
body: The Battle of Midway inflicted Japan’s first decisive defeat at sea in the Second World War.

title: Last major RAAF air combat of the Second World War
body: No. 78 Squadron fought a major air action south of Biak during operations in New Guinea.

title: Cyprus reinforced by Australian troops
body: Australian troops reinforced Cyprus after the fighting in Crete.

title: D-Day landings in Normandy
body: Allied forces landed in Normandy, opening a third front against Germany in Europe.

title: Battle of Binh Ba
body: Australian forces fought a major action at Binh Ba in South Vietnam.

title: First day of the Battle of the Somme
body: The Battle of the Somme began with the worst single day in the history of British arms.

title: Sinking of the Montevideo Maru
body: The Montevideo Maru was sunk with more than 1,050 Australian prisoners aboard, Australia’s worst maritime disaster.

title: 7th Division lands at Balikpapan
body: The Balikpapan landing was the largest and final Australian amphibious landing of the Second World War.

title: Private H. Dalziel wins the Victoria Cross
body: Private H. Dalziel won the Victoria Cross at Hamel Wood in France.

title: Acting Wing Commander H. Edwards wins the Victoria Cross
body: Acting Wing Commander H. Edwards earned the Victoria Cross in a raid on Bremen.

title: Death of Prime Minister Curtin
body: Prime Minister John Curtin, who led Australia through the darkest years of the Pacific war, died in office.

title: Corporal W.E. Brown wins the Victoria Cross
body: Corporal W.E. Brown won the Victoria Cross at Villers-Bretonneux.

title: Battle of Damour
body: Australian troops fought the final battle in Lebanon against Vichy French forces at Damour.

title: Last major action involving Australians at Tobruk
body: Australian forces fought one of their last major actions in the Tobruk siege before withdrawal.

title: Iraq invades Kuwait
body: Iraq’s invasion of Kuwait began the chain of events that led to the Gulf War of 1991.

title: Second Maori War begins
body: Australians and colonial connections became involved in fighting in New Zealand during the Second Maori War.

title: Australia offers assistance to Great Britain
body: With war in Europe imminent, Australia offered naval support and to raise a force of 20,000 troops.

title: Atomic bomb dropped on Hiroshima
body: The first atomic bomb was dropped on Hiroshima, transforming the final phase of the Pacific war.

title: Australians charge at the Nek
body: Light Horse regiments attacked Ottoman positions at the Nek during the August Offensive at Gallipoli.

title: Australian contingents sail for China
body: New South Wales and Victorian contingents sailed for China in connection with the Boxer Rebellion.

title: Lance Corporal L. Keysor wins the Victoria Cross
body: Lance Corporal Leonard Keysor won the Victoria Cross at Lone Pine, Gallipoli.

title: Trooper J.H. Bisdee wins the Victoria Cross
body: Trooper J.H. Bisdee won the Victoria Cross at Warm Bad in South Africa.

title: Lieutenant G.G. Wylly wins the Victoria Cross
body: Lieutenant G.G. Wylly won the Victoria Cross at Warm Bad in South Africa.

title: Temporary Corporal A.H. Buckley wins the Victoria Cross
body: A.H. Buckley received the Victoria Cross for action at Péronne.

title: Private W.M. Currey wins the Victoria Cross
body: W.M. Currey received the Victoria Cross for action at Péronne.

title: Sergeant A.D. Lowerson wins the Victoria Cross
body: A.D. Lowerson won the Victoria Cross at Mont St Quentin.

title: Private R. Mactier wins the Victoria Cross
body: R. Mactier won the Victoria Cross at Mont St Quentin.

title: Lieutenant E.T. Towner wins the Victoria Cross
body: E.T. Towner won the Victoria Cross at Mont St Quentin.

title: Temporary Corporal L.C. Weathers wins the Victoria Cross
body: L.C. Weathers won the Victoria Cross at Péronne.

title: Australian Light Horse takes Damascus
body: Australian Light Horse forces advanced into Damascus at the end of the Middle East campaign.

title: 9th Division captures Finschhafen
body: The capture of Finschhafen helped open the Huon Peninsula campaign in New Guinea.

title: Lieutenant J. Maxwell performs VC action
body: Lieutenant J. Maxwell performed the action that resulted in the Victoria Cross near Estrees, France.

title: Australian Vietnam Veterans’ National Memorial dedicated
body: The Australian Vietnam Veterans’ National Memorial in Canberra was dedicated.

title: HMAS Sydney in Korean waters
body: HMAS Sydney began carrier patrols off Korea, making Australia the third nation to operate a carrier there.

title: Second Australian battalion committed to Korea
body: The Australian Government announced that a second battalion would join the Korean War commitment.

title: HMAS Sydney at Vung Tau
body: HMAS Sydney embarked 3RAR as Australia’s participation in the Vietnam War wound down.

title: UN forces authorised to cross the 38th parallel
body: UN command forces were authorised to pursue North Korean troops beyond the 38th parallel.

title: First AIF sails
body: The first Australian and New Zealand contingent sailed from Albany for Egypt.

title: Australia ratifies the United Nations Charter
body: Australia ratified the UN Charter after playing a leading role in the organisation’s formation.

title: No. 77 Squadron awarded South Korean Presidential Citation
body: No. 77 Squadron RAAF received a Republic of South Korea Presidential Citation for its service in Korea.

title: National Army Memorial dedicated
body: The National Army Memorial on Anzac Parade in Canberra was dedicated.

title: Kokoda airstrip re-occupied
body: The re-occupation of Kokoda airstrip enabled the Australian advance across the Owen Stanley Range to continue.

title: Armistice with Austria-Hungary signed
body: The armistice with Austria-Hungary left Germany to face the Allies alone in the final days of the First World War.

title: Troops land at Jacquinot Bay
body: Australian troops landed at Jacquinot Bay, New Britain, creating an important operational base.

title: Battle of Pakchon
body: 3RAR fought and held key features overlooking Pakchon in Korea.

title: HMAS Armidale sunk
body: HMAS Armidale was sunk by Japanese aircraft near Timor; Teddy Sheean continued firing as the ship went down.

title: Air battle over Sunchon
body: RAAF Meteors fought the largest air-to-air battle by the RAAF in the Korean War.

title: First Australian military aircraft despatched to war
body: Australian aircraft and crews departed to form the Mesopotamian Half Flight.

title: Siege of Kut begins in Mesopotamia
body: Australian flying personnel serving with the Royal Flying Corps became trapped in Kut when the town was besieged.

title: First AIF disembarks in Egypt
body: Australian troops disembarked in Egypt after plans changed from Britain to guarding the Suez Canal.

title: No. 77 Squadron returns from Korea
body: No. 77 Squadron returned after distinguished service flying Mustangs and Meteors in Korea.

title: RAN involvement in Persian Gulf approved
body: The Australian Government approved Royal Australian Navy involvement in the Persian Gulf.

title: First official RAAF operation of the Second World War
body: No. 10 Squadron flew the first official RAAF operation of the Second World War.

title: Anzac Day
body: Anzac Day is Australia’s national day of remembrance for those who served and died in wars and war-like conflicts.

title: Remembrance Day
body: Remembrance Day commemorates the armistice that ended fighting in the First World War at the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month.

title: NSW Police Force formally established
body: The Police Regulation Act combined earlier watch and police systems into the NSW Police Force.

title: NSW Rural Fire Service established
body: The Rural Fires Act 1997 formally established the NSW Rural Fire Service.

title: First Australian troops arrive at Tobruk
body: Australian forces moved into Tobruk as the North African campaign accelerated.

title: Guadalcanal campaign nears its end
body: Allied forces tightened their hold on Guadalcanal during the campaign that shifted momentum in the Pacific.

title: Australians continue pursuit in Libya
body: Australian formations pressed west after early successes against Italian positions in North Africa.

title: Australian naval patrols continue in Korea
body: Royal Australian Navy ships maintained bombardment and patrol tasks during the Korean War.

title: Pressure grows on Rabaul garrison
body: Japanese attacks intensified against the Rabaul garrison before its eventual fall later that month.

title: RAAF Meteors continue Korean operations
body: RAAF Meteors sustained offensive and defensive air operations as the Korean War widened.

title: Gallipoli campaign lessons studied
body: Australian commanders assessed the Gallipoli campaign as the AIF prepared for new operations in Egypt and France.

title: Australian troops face mounting pressure in Malaya
body: Australian units in Malaya continued fighting delaying actions during the Japanese advance.

title: Battle for Singapore intensifies
body: Japanese assaults intensified as Allied forces prepared the defence of Singapore.

title: Japanese land on Singapore Island
body: The Japanese assault on Singapore Island placed Australian units under immediate and severe pressure.

title: Australian lines contract on Singapore
body: Defenders withdrew and regrouped as fighting spread across Singapore Island.

title: Australian units continue preparations in the Middle East
body: Australian troops in the Middle East continued training and preparations before later campaigns in Greece, Syria and North Africa.

title: Water and ammunition shortages deepen at Singapore
body: Conditions worsened for Allied defenders as the battle for Singapore approached its final stage.

title: Singapore defence reaches crisis point
body: Allied positions became increasingly fragile as the Japanese pressed attacks around Singapore.

title: Northern Australia braces for attack
body: The threat to northern Australia sharpened as Japanese operations expanded southward.

title: Final day before Singapore’s surrender
body: Australian and Allied troops fought on despite rapidly deteriorating circumstances before the surrender.

title: Japanese advances deepen through the islands
body: Japanese advances through the islands north of Australia continued after the fall of Rabaul and Singapore.

title: Aftermath of Java Sea fighting
body: Survivors and remnants of Allied naval forces scattered after the fighting around Java.

title: Attention turns to New Guinea approaches
body: Australia’s northern approaches became the focus as Japanese expansion threatened New Guinea.

title: New Guinea campaign conditions develop
body: Terrain, climate and logistics began shaping what would become the New Guinea campaigns.

title: Sea lanes under increasing pressure
body: Japanese operations placed Allied shipping routes in the region under serious threat.

title: Australian mobilisation accelerates
body: Australia intensified mobilisation and defence preparations as the Pacific war moved closer.

title: Australian contingents continue in South Africa
body: Australian colonial contingents continued serving in the South African War.

title: Pacific war reshapes Australia’s strategy
body: Australian strategy increasingly centred on the defence of the mainland and nearby island chains.

title: Australian commanders prepare for counter-strokes in France
body: Australian formations continued preparations that would later culminate in major actions on the Western Front.

title: German offensives continue in the north
body: The German spring offensives continued to threaten Allied positions on the Western Front.

title: AIF prepares in Egypt for Gallipoli
body: Australian troops in Egypt made final preparations before the Gallipoli landings later that month.

title: Defence of Tobruk takes shape
body: Australian troops and allies prepared Tobruk’s perimeter against the coming siege.

title: Australian troops fight in Greece
body: Australian formations became heavily engaged during the campaign in Greece.

title: Australian battalion operations continue in Korea
body: Australian infantry units continued offensive and defensive operations during the Korean War.

title: New Guinea operations continue after Kokoda
body: Australian operations in New Guinea continued after the Kokoda campaign into other hard-fought phases.

title: Allied staging bases support final Pacific campaigns
body: Forward staging areas supported the final Allied offensives in Borneo and the Netherlands East Indies.

title: Coral Sea fighting intensifies
body: Carrier aircraft and escorting fleets clashed as the Battle of the Coral Sea intensified.

title: Battle of the Coral Sea reaches decisive phase
body: The battle helped check the Japanese advance toward Port Moresby.

title: Victory celebrations spread after VE Day
body: The defeat of Germany marked a major turning point even as Australian forces remained committed in the Pacific.

title: Australian divisions continue Hindenburg Line fighting
body: Heavy fighting continued as Australian units attacked strong German positions in France.

title: Anzac perimeter consolidation continues
body: After the landing, Australian troops continued consolidating the Anzac position under fire.

title: New Guinea defences remain central
body: Attention remained focused on New Guinea and the defence of Port Moresby after Coral Sea.

title: Tarakan operations continue
body: Australian troops continued operations on Tarakan after the initial landings.

title: Australian task force operations continue in Phuoc Tuy
body: Australian troops continued patrolling and security operations in Phuoc Tuy province during the Vietnam War.

title: Normandy bridgehead expands
body: Allied forces consolidated positions ashore after the D-Day landings.

title: Syria–Lebanon campaign begins
body: Australian forces joined the Allied invasion of Syria and Lebanon against Vichy French forces.

title: Australian fighting continues in Syria and Lebanon
body: Australian troops pressed into difficult country during the Syria–Lebanon campaign.

title: RAAF air operations continue in Korea
body: No. 77 Squadron maintained combat air operations in the Korean theatre.

title: Siege of Tobruk continues
body: Australian troops remained under pressure during the prolonged siege of Tobruk.

title: Australian service continues across Middle East theatres
body: Australian formations were spread across multiple theatres including Tobruk and Syria.

title: Australian patrol activity continues in Vietnam
body: Patrolling and village-security tasks remained central to Australian operations in Vietnam.

title: Allied build-up in France continues
body: The Allied lodgement in Normandy grew as reinforcements and supplies poured ashore.

title: Preparations complete for Hamel
body: Australian and American troops completed preparations for the successful attack at Hamel.

title: Battle of Hamel
body: The Battle of Hamel became one of the AIF’s most celebrated and carefully planned victories on the Western Front.

title: Fighting continues near Beirut and Damour
body: Australian and allied troops continued their advance in the Syria–Lebanon campaign.

title: Allied pressure builds in the south-west Pacific
body: Campaigns in New Guinea and the islands continued to shift momentum toward the Allies.

title: Damour campaign concludes
body: The fall of Damour helped secure success in the Syria–Lebanon campaign.

title: Aftermath of the Montevideo Maru sinking felt across Australia
body: Families and communities gradually learned the scale of losses after the Montevideo Maru disaster.

title: Japanese pressure grows toward Kokoda
body: Japanese moves in Papua increased the danger to Kokoda and Port Moresby.

title: Allied strategy increasingly centres on New Guinea
body: New Guinea’s strategic importance continued to rise in Allied planning.

title: Lone Pine fighting continues
body: Australian forces continued fierce close fighting at Lone Pine during the August Offensive.

title: August Offensive broadens at Gallipoli
body: Operations spread across the Gallipoli front as the August Offensive continued.

title: Atomic warfare shocks the world
body: The use of atomic bombs transformed the final days of the Pacific war.

title: Kokoda campaign intensifies
body: Fighting along the Kokoda Track intensified as Australian forces tried to slow the Japanese advance.

title: Milne Bay preparations continue
body: Allied forces prepared the Milne Bay defences before the Japanese attempt to seize the area.

title: Japan moves toward surrender
body: Events rushed toward the end of the Pacific war as Japan accepted surrender terms.

title: Victory in the Pacific celebrations begin
body: News of Japan’s surrender triggered celebrations while occupation and repatriation tasks still lay ahead.

title: Australian task force operations continue before Long Tan
body: Australian troops in Phuoc Tuy province were engaged in operations just before the Battle of Long Tan.

title: Australian advance continues on Western Front
body: Australian formations maintained pressure on retreating German forces in the final months of the war.

title: Australia’s northern approaches remain contested
body: 1942 remained the pivotal year in the defence of Australia and its northern approaches.

title: Aftermath of Mont St Quentin
body: Australian troops consolidated gains after the fighting at Mont St Quentin and Péronne.

title: Australian operations continue in Phuoc Tuy
body: Australian battalions continued patrolling, cordon and search, and security tasks in South Vietnam.

title: Light Horse continues Middle East advance
body: Australian mounted troops continued preparations for the decisive advance in Palestine.

title: Occupation and repatriation tasks begin after surrender
body: With hostilities ending, Australian forces shifted toward occupation, recovery and repatriation responsibilities.

title: RAN transitions to post-war duties
body: The RAN moved from combat operations toward transport, occupation and recovery tasks after Japan’s surrender.

title: RAAF focuses on recovery and transport tasks
body: RAAF priorities shifted toward movement, recovery and support tasks as the war ended.

title: Australian mounted forces continue in Syria
body: After Damascus, Australian mounted troops continued moving north during the final Middle East operations.

title: UN advance continues in Korea
body: UN forces continued offensive operations after being authorised to cross the 38th parallel.

title: Canberra’s memorial landscape continues to grow
body: National memorials in Canberra increasingly reflected the breadth of Australia’s service history.

title: Huon Peninsula operations continue
body: Australian forces continued operations after the capture of Finschhafen on the Huon Peninsula.

title: Australian air and naval efforts continue in Korea
body: Australian air and naval units maintained pressure and support in Korean operations.

title: Australian withdrawal from Vietnam advances
body: Australia’s drawdown from Vietnam continued as combat responsibilities reduced.

title: Modern commemoration of Australian soldiers grows
body: National commemorative practice increasingly recognised the breadth of Australian Army service.

title: Public commemoration of Vietnam service strengthens
body: By the late 1980s, national recognition of Vietnam veterans had become more visible in Australian public life.

title: Australian advance from Kokoda continues
body: Australian forces pushed on after reoccupying Kokoda, pursuing Japanese troops northward.

title: Pakchon fighting remembered
body: The Battle of Pakchon became one of the notable early Australian actions of the Korean War.

title: Jacquinot Bay build-up continues
body: Australian forces continued developing Jacquinot Bay as a base on New Britain.

title: Final days before the Armistice
body: Australian troops remained engaged in the closing phase of the First World War.

title: Europe awaits the Armistice
body: Fighting and negotiations converged in the final day before the Armistice of 11 November 1918.

title: After the Armistice
body: Celebration, relief and reflection followed the Armistice, even as occupation and recovery tasks remained.

title: Post-war transition continues
body: Australian service personnel increasingly shifted to occupation, transport and repatriation duties after the war.

title: RAAF service in Korea continues
body: Australian airmen continued fighter and ground-attack operations in Korea.

title: HMAS Armidale sacrifice remembered
body: The loss of HMAS Armidale remained one of the RAN’s most poignant wartime tragedies.

title: Pacific War widens dramatically
body: The opening of war across the Pacific transformed Australia’s strategic environment.

title: War begins in Malaya
body: Australian troops became engaged in the Malayan campaign after Japan opened war in the region.

title: Naval war expands in Asian waters
body: Naval conflict rapidly spread through the South China Sea and surrounding waters.

title: Loss of Prince of Wales and Repulse
body: The sinking of the British capital ships transformed the naval balance in South-East Asia.

title: Australian troops continue fighting in Malaya
body: Australian battalions fought delaying actions as Japanese forces pressed southward in Malaya.

title: North African campaign continues
body: Commonwealth operations in Libya continued during the campaign against Italian positions.

title: Post-war naval responsibilities continue
body: The RAN remained heavily engaged in transport, occupation and recovery roles after the war.

title: Night Watch formed in early Sydney
body: NSW Police history traces the first civilian police force in Australia to the Night Watch formed in 1789.

title: Women first recruited by NSW Police
body: NSW Police history records the advertising of the first positions for female police in 1915.

title: Bombing of Darwin commemorated
body: DVA recognises 19 February as the anniversary of the Bombing of Darwin, the first direct attack on the Australian mainland.

title: Vietnam Veterans’ Day
body: DVA commemorates Vietnam Veterans’ Day on 18 August, the anniversary of the Battle of Long Tan.

title: NSW Fire Brigades renamed Fire and Rescue NSW
body: On 1 January 2011, NSW Fire Brigades changed its name to Fire and Rescue NSW to reflect its broader all-hazards role.

title: Marine Rescue NSW launched operations
body: Marine Rescue NSW members took to the water and airwaves as a unified volunteer marine rescue service on 1 January 2010.

title: Australian honours system formally began
body: The Australian honours system formally began on 14 February 1975.

title: Order of Australia instituted
body: The Order of Australia was instituted on 14 February 1975 as an Australian society of honour.

title: Australian Bravery Decorations instituted
body: The Australian Bravery Decorations were instituted by Letters Patent dated 14 February 1975.

title: National Medal introduced
body: The National Medal was introduced as part of the Australian honours system on 14 February 1975.

title: Metropolitan Fire Brigade established
body: The Metropolitan Fire Brigade, precursor to Fire and Rescue NSW, was established on 14 February 1884.

title: Victoria State Emergency Service established under the VICSES name
body: On 5 March 1975 the earlier Civil Defence Organisation was renamed the Victoria State Emergency Service (VICSES).

title: NSW Ambulance origins begin with Civil Ambulance and Transport Brigade
body: NSW Ambulance traces its origins to 1 April 1895 with the establishment of the Civil Ambulance and Transport Brigade.

title: Country Fire Authority officially formed
body: CFA marks its official formation on 2 April 1945.

title: Australian Border Force established
body: On 1 July 2015 the Australian Border Force became the operational arm of the integrated border and immigration portfolio.

title: Marine Rescue NSW formally established
body: Marine Rescue NSW was formally established on 1 July 2009 before commencing unified operations the following year.

title: Queensland’s first ambulance service begins
body: The City Ambulance Transport Brigade, Queensland’s first ambulance service, began on 12 September 1892.

title: Australian Federal Police commenced operations
body: The Australian Federal Police commenced operations on 19 October 1979.

title: Australian Intelligence Corps formed
body: The Australian Intelligence Corps was formed on 6 December 1907 and is one of the oldest Commonwealth intelligence corps.

title: Federal Capital Territory proclaimed
body: A police presence was established in the Canberra district as the Federal Capital Territory was proclaimed.

title: Commonwealth Police Force established by regulation
body: The first Commonwealth Police Force was established by regulation under the War Precautions framework in late November 1917.

title: Commonwealth Police Force regulation replaced
body: The original regulation establishing the first Commonwealth Police Force was rescinded and replaced with a new version in December 1917.

title: Special Intelligence Bureau responsibility passed to Attorney-General’s Department
body: Responsibility for the Special Intelligence Bureau passed to the Attorney-General’s Department on 1 March 1919.

title: Investigation Branch formed
body: The Investigation Branch was formed by amalgamating the Special Intelligence Bureau and the first Commonwealth Police Force.

title: First policeman stationed in Canberra
body: Constable John W. Stuart arrived at Molonglo as the first policeman stationed in Canberra.

title: Bill introduced for a new Commonwealth law-enforcement organisation
body: A Bill was introduced authorising the formation of a new Commonwealth law-enforcement organisation.

title: Peace Officers Act 1925 receives Royal Assent
body: Royal Assent was given to the Peace Officers Act 1925, empowering the Attorney-General to appoint peace officers.

title: H. E. Jones appointed Superintending Peace Officer
body: Major Harold Edward Jones was appointed Superintending Peace Officer and authorised to appoint peace officers and special peace officers.

title: Canberra police quarters and office arrangements confirmed
body: The Home and Territories Department advised on police quarters, office space and station arrangements in Canberra.

title: Police Station Canberra established
body: Police Station Canberra was established in the Federal Capital Territory.

title: Parliament House opened in Canberra with police assigned
body: Parliament House was opened in Canberra and uniformed police and detectives from all States were assigned for duty.

title: Cabinet agreed to establish a Federal Capital Territory police force
body: Cabinet agreed to establish a Federal Capital Territory police force to enforce local laws and guard Government House.

title: Commonwealth Police in the Federal Capital Territory formalised
body: An ordinance provided for the establishment of a police force in the Federal Capital Territory under Major H. E. Jones.

title: Molonglo police station abandoned
body: The former police station at Molonglo was abandoned when new quarters were opened at Acton.

title: First court in Canberra opened
body: The first court in Canberra, located at a former Acton home, was opened.

title: Planning for Defence Establishments Guard accelerated
body: Government approved the proposal to establish the Defence Establishments Guard after security concerns at munitions sites.

title: Cecil Welch appointed to recruit the Defence Establishments Guard
body: Cecil Welch was appointed Sub-Inspector and began recruiting the new guarding force.

title: Defence Establishments Guard officially formed
body: The Defence Establishments Guard was officially formed and its first guards were sworn in as Commonwealth Peace Officers.

title: Guard doubled as war became imminent
body: The guard was rapidly expanded on the eve of the Second World War as additional special peace officers were sworn in.

title: Conference recommended new funding model for the Peace Officers Guard
body: A Melbourne conference recommended that Peace Officers Guard costs move to the Attorney-General’s Department.

title: Special peace officers made eligible for permanent appointment
body: A wartime measure allowed special peace officers who completed three months of service to become permanent peace officers.

title: Security Service formed within the Attorney-General’s Department
body: The Security Service was formed to assume national security activities previously carried out by the Army.

title: Defence Establishments Guard renamed Peace Officers Guard
body: The Defence Establishments Guard officially changed its name to the Peace Officers Guard.

title: Harold Edward Jones retired
body: Colonel Harold Edward Jones retired and Colonel Eric Longfield Lloyd became Superintending Peace Officer.

title: Commonwealth Investigation Service formed
body: The Commonwealth Investigation Branch restructured and merged with the wartime Military Security Service to form the Commonwealth Investigation Service.

title: First policewoman appointed to ACT Police
body: Alice Clarke became the first policewoman appointed to the ACT Police.

title: Proposal put forward to combine the Peace Officers Guard and CIS
body: The Attorney-General’s Department proposed combining the Peace Officers Guard and the Commonwealth Investigation Service.

title: Government approved integrated Commonwealth Police Force
body: The Government approved the integration of the Commonwealth Investigation Service and the Peace Officers Guard into a new Commonwealth Police Force.

title: Bill introduced for Commonwealth Police Force
body: The enabling Bill for the formation of the Commonwealth Police Force was introduced in the Senate.

title: Commonwealth Police Force formally established
body: The Commonwealth Police Force was formally established and Ray Whitrod was appointed Commissioner.

title: Agreement signed for North West Cape communications complex
body: Prime Minister Menzies signed the agreement for the American communications complex at North West Cape, later guarded by Commonwealth Police.

title: L. W. Powley appointed ACT Police Commissioner
body: L. W. Powley was appointed Commissioner of ACT Police, replacing E. Richards.

title: Special Inter-Departmental Committee on Counter-Terrorism established
body: The Special Inter-Departmental Committee on Counter-Terrorism was established to coordinate anti-terror advice and security precautions.

title: ‘Australia Police’ appeared as replacement name
body: The term ‘Australia Police’ appeared as the proposed replacement name for the Commonwealth Police Force.

title: Legislation introduced to form Australia Police
body: Legislation to form a new Australia Police was introduced into Parliament.

title: Australia Police legislation lapsed after Parliament prorogued
body: The Bill to form Australia Police lapsed when Parliament was prorogued after the dismissal of the Whitlam Government.

title: Sydney Hilton bombing
body: A bomb exploded outside the Hilton Hotel in Sydney, becoming a pivotal event in Commonwealth protective-security reform.

title: Sir Robert Mark commissioned to review Commonwealth policing
body: Sir Robert Mark was commissioned to review Commonwealth police resources, protective security and counter-terrorist measures.

title: Government accepted recommendations to create AFP
body: The Government announced acceptance of recommendations leading to creation of the Australian Federal Police.

title: First AFP Commissioner announced
body: The Government announced Sir Colin Woods as the first Commissioner for the new Australian Federal Police.

title: Australian Federal Police enabling legislation introduced
body: The Minister for Administrative Services introduced the enabling legislation for the Australian Federal Police.

title: Australian Federal Police Act 1979 received Royal Assent
body: The Australian Federal Police Act 1979 received Royal Assent, paving the way for the AFP’s commencement.

title: AFP Complex at Weston opened
body: The Australian Federal Police Complex at Weston was opened.

title: Australian Bureau of Criminal Intelligence established
body: The Australian Bureau of Criminal Intelligence was established to facilitate criminal intelligence exchange between police services in Australia.

title: Sir Colin Woods retired as AFP Commissioner
body: Sir Colin Woods retired as Commissioner of the Australian Federal Police.

title: Major-General Ronald Grey began duty as AFP Commissioner
body: Major-General Ronald Grey began duty as AFP Commissioner.

title: National Crime Authority created
body: The National Crime Authority was created.

title: Australian Federal Police (Amendment) Act 1984 received Royal Assent
body: The Act establishing the Australian Protective Service received Royal Assent.

title: Australian Protective Service officially established
body: The Australian Protective Service was officially established, with Peter Dawson appointed first Director.

title: Australian Protective Service Act 1987 received Royal Assent
body: The Australian Protective Service Act 1987 received Royal Assent.

title: Australian Protective Service Act 1987 commenced
body: The Australian Protective Service Act 1987 came into operation.

title: Peter McAulay appointed AFP Commissioner
body: Peter McAulay was appointed Commissioner of the Australian Federal Police.

title: Assistant Commissioner Colin Winchester killed
body: Assistant Commissioner Colin Winchester was killed; the investigation was code-named Operation Peat.

title: APS National Training Centre at Kingston opened
body: The Australian Protective Service National Training Centre at Kingston was opened.

title: Airport counter-terror first-response role transfer began
body: Responsibility for the counter-terrorist first-response role at major airports began transferring from the AFP to the APS.

title: APS moved to West Block
body: The Australian Protective Service moved to West Block as responsibility shifted within government.

title: Mick Palmer sworn in as AFP Commissioner
body: Mick Palmer was sworn in as Commissioner of the Australian Federal Police.

title: 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment arrives in Vietnam
body: The 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment arrived in South Vietnam under the US 173rd Brigade.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 161st Battery RNZA arrives in Vietnam
body: 161st Battery, Royal New Zealand Artillery, arrived in theatre to support allied operations in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 105th Field Battery and supporting Australian elements arrive in Vietnam
body: 105th Field Battery RAA, 1st APC Troop and 161 Reconnaissance Flight elements arrived in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1st Australian Task Force established in Vietnam
body: 1st Australian Task Force was established, with headquarters at Nui Dat in Phuoc Tuy Province.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1RAR first tour ends in Vietnam
body: 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment departed after its initial tour in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1st APC Squadron redesignated A Squadron, 3rd Cavalry Regiment
body: The 1st APC Squadron was redesignated A Squadron, 3rd Cavalry Regiment in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 2RAR and 7RAR arrive with major 1ATF reinforcement
body: 2nd and 7th Battalions, RAR, 4th Field Regiment RAA and 1st SAS Squadron elements arrived in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: HMAS Hobart begins Vietnam deployment
body: HMAS Hobart joined the Seventh Fleet for naval gunfire and screening duties off Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: No. 2 Squadron RAAF arrives at Phan Rang
body: No. 2 Squadron RAAF with Canberra aircraft arrived at Phan Rang for operations in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: V Company, New Zealand Infantry, arrives
body: V Company of the New Zealand infantry contingent arrived to serve with Australian forces in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: RAN Helicopter Flight Vietnam arrives
body: The Royal Australian Navy Helicopter Flight Vietnam arrived to serve with a US assault helicopter company.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 3RAR arrives in Vietnam
body: 3rd Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment arrived for service with 1ATF in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: W Company, New Zealand Infantry, arrives
body: W Company of the New Zealand infantry contingent arrived in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 1st Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment arrived for its second Vietnam tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 4RAR and major supporting elements arrive in Vietnam
body: 4RAR, 12th Field Regiment RAA, C Squadron/1st Armoured Regiment and 2nd SAS Squadron elements arrived.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 9RAR arrives in Vietnam
body: 9th Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment arrived for service with 1ATF.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: New Zealand SAS troop arrives in Vietnam
body: A New Zealand SAS troop arrived to serve alongside Australian forces in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 5RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 5th Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment returned to Vietnam for a second tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 3 SAS Squadron begins second Vietnam tour
body: 3 SAS Squadron returned to Vietnam for its second tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: B Squadron, 1st Armoured Regiment arrives
body: B Squadron, 1st Armoured Regiment arrived in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1st Field Regiment begins second Vietnam tour
body: 1st Field Regiment RAA arrived for its second Vietnam tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 6RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 6th Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment returned for a second Vietnam tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: B Squadron, 3rd Cavalry Regiment arrives
body: B Squadron, 3rd Cavalry Regiment arrived in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1 SAS Squadron begins second Vietnam tour
body: 1 SAS Squadron returned to Vietnam for its second tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 7RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 7th Battalion, Royal Australian Regiment returned for a second Vietnam tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 2RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 2RAR arrived for its second Vietnam tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: V Company, New Zealand, withdrawn from Vietnam
body: V Company of the New Zealand infantry contingent was withdrawn during Australia and New Zealand’s drawdown.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 4RAR returns to Vietnam for second tour
body: 4RAR arrived in Vietnam for its second tour.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: RAN Helicopter Flight Vietnam departs
body: The Royal Australian Navy Helicopter Flight Vietnam departed after its service in theatre.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 3RAR departs Vietnam
body: 3RAR departed Vietnam during the late phase of the Australian drawdown.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: 1st Australian Task Force departs Vietnam
body: Headquarters 1 ATF, 4RAR and A Squadron/3rd Cavalry departed Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: Australian Army Assistance Group departs Vietnam
body: The Australian Army Assistance Group departed, ending the final Australian Army presence in Vietnam.
sourceLabel: Australian and New Zealand Forces in Vietnam

title: Vinegar Hill rebellion suppressed
body: The battle at Vinegar Hill near Parramatta marked the suppression of the major Irish convict uprising in New South Wales.

title: Eureka Stockade
body: The battle at the Eureka Stockade became a defining event in colonial political history and democratic reform.

title: HMAS Sydney defeats SMS Emden
body: HMAS Sydney fought and defeated the German raider Emden in the RAN’s first major single-ship action.

title: Battle of Courtney’s Post
body: Australian defenders repulsed a major Turkish counter-attack at Courtney’s Post on Gallipoli.

title: Battle of Fromelles begins
body: The Battle of Fromelles began, producing the worst one-day Australian death toll in history.

title: First Battle of Bullecourt
body: The First Battle of Bullecourt began with Australian forces attacking the Hindenburg Line.

title: Battle of Messines
body: The Battle of Messines opened with a massive mine detonation and a successful Allied assault including Australian troops.

title: Passchendaele
body: Australian forces fought in the mire and heavy losses of Passchendaele.

title: Battle for Singapore begins
body: The climactic battle for Singapore opened as Japanese forces landed on Singapore Island.

title: Japanese midget-sub attack on Sydney Harbour
body: Japanese midget submarines attacked Sydney Harbour in one of the most dramatic attacks on the Australian mainland war effort.

title: First Battle of El Alamein begins
body: The First Battle of El Alamein opened as Allied forces, including Australians, checked the Axis advance into Egypt.

title: Second Battle of El Alamein begins
body: The Second Battle of El Alamein began, leading to one of the decisive Allied victories of the war in North Africa.

title: Battle of Kapyong begins
body: Australian troops fought at Kapyong as the communist advance in Korea was halted.

title: Battle of Coral–Balmoral begins
body: The Battle of Coral–Balmoral began and became one of Australia’s biggest Vietnam battles.

title: Australian success in Iraq operation highlighted
body: The battle-history source marks 30 January 2005 as a significant Australian operational success in Iraq.

title: Afghanistan battle that produced Australian Victoria Cross recognition
body: The battle-history source highlights 11 June 2010 as a key Australian action in Afghanistan.

title: Ash Wednesday bushfires
body: Ash Wednesday bushfires struck Victoria and South Australia, killing 75 people and driving major reform in bushfire command, communications and inter-service coordination.

title: Cyclone Tracy strikes Darwin
body: Cyclone Tracy struck Darwin on Christmas Eve 1974, killing 71 people and devastating the city, prompting major changes to cyclone-proof building standards.

title: Granville rail disaster
body: The Granville rail disaster killed 83 people and injured 210, leading to bridge redesign and reinforcement measures.

title: Pacific Adventurer oil spill affects Queensland coast
body: An oil spill from the Pacific Adventurer affected beaches and wildlife along the Queensland coast and prompted stronger preventative measures and investigation action.

title: Broome attacked during Australia’s home defence campaign
body: Japanese bombers attacked Broome, destroying aircraft carrying evacuees from the Netherlands East Indies and causing heavy casualties.

title: Japanese submarine shells Sydney suburbs
body: A Japanese mother submarine shelled Sydney’s eastern suburbs a week after the midget-submarine raid, causing damage but no deaths.

title: Canberra Fire Brigade incorporated under NSW Board of Fire Commissioners
body: On 4 April 1958 the NSW Board of Fire Commissioners incorporated the Canberra Fire Brigade.

title: Ainslie Fire Station opened
body: Ainslie Fire Station opened as part of the expansion of Canberra’s fire service.

title: Phillip Fire Station opened
body: Phillip Fire Station opened as Canberra’s fire service expanded southward.

title: Belconnen Fire Station opened
body: Belconnen Fire Station opened to support the growing north Canberra region.

title: Tuggeranong (Kambah) Fire Station opened
body: The Tuggeranong (Kambah) fire station opened as the ACT brigade expanded into the south.

title: Charnwood Fire Station opened
body: Charnwood Fire Station opened as part of continued ACT fire service growth.

title: Forrest station relocated to Fyshwick
body: The original Forrest station was relocated to Fyshwick on 31 March 1983.

title: Chisholm Fire Station opened
body: Chisholm Fire Station opened as the ACT brigade continued expanding.

title: Greenway Fire Station opened
body: Greenway Fire Station opened to support the growing Tuggeranong district.

title: Gungahlin Fire Station opened
body: Gungahlin Fire Station opened as Canberra’s northern suburbs expanded.

title: Australian Special Intelligence Bureau established
body: An Australian branch of the Imperial Counter Espionage Bureau was established in January 1916 as the Australian Special Intelligence Bureau.

title: Investigation Branch succeeds the Commonwealth Police Force and SIB
body: In 1919 the Commonwealth Police Force and the Special Intelligence Bureau merged to form the Investigation Branch.

title: Western Australia Police Force formed
body: The Western Australia Police Force traces its formation to 1 January 1834.

title: Western Australian Police Ordinance passed
body: The colony’s Legislative Council passed a Police Ordinance outlining police powers and responsibilities.

title: Municipal police force established at Port Dalrymple
body: A municipal police force was established at Port Dalrymple (Launceston), with Thomas Massey as Chief Constable.

title: Unified Tasmania Police force came into being
body: With the Police Regulation Act 1898, one unified Tasmania police force came into being on 1 January 1899.

title: South Australia Police formed
body: South Australia established Australia’s first centrally organised police force on 28 April 1838.

title: Queensland Police Force established
body: The Queensland Police Force was established on 1 January 1864 with 287 officers.

title: Queensland Detective Office created
body: The Detective Office was created on 1 December 1864 with Sub-Inspector George Elliott in charge.

title: NSW Police Force established under the Police Regulation Act
body: On 1 March 1862, existing police forces amalgamated to establish the NSW Police Force under Inspector General John McLerie.

title: Clive William Graham appointed Commissioner of NT Police
body: On 1 July 1964 Clive William Graham was appointed Commissioner and the force was administered as part of the Northern Territory Public Service.

title: Lieutenant General Sir Henry Wells became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: Lieutenant General Sir Henry Wells began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: Vice-Admiral Sir Roy Dowling became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: Vice-Admiral Sir Roy Dowling began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: Air Chief Marshal Sir Frederick Scherger became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: Air Chief Marshal Sir Frederick Scherger began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: General Sir John Wilton became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: General Sir John Wilton began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: Admiral Sir Victor Smith became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: Admiral Sir Victor Smith began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: General Sir Francis Hassett became Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee
body: General Sir Francis Hassett began his term as Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee.

title: General Sir Francis Hassett became Chief of Defence Force Staff
body: General Sir Francis Hassett began his term as Chief of Defence Force Staff.

title: General Sir Arthur MacDonald became Chief of Defence Force Staff
body: General Sir Arthur MacDonald began his term as Chief of Defence Force Staff.

title: Admiral Sir Anthony Synnot became Chief of Defence Force Staff
body: Admiral Sir Anthony Synnot began his term as Chief of Defence Force Staff.

title: Air Chief Marshal Sir Neville McNamara became Chief of Defence Force Staff
body: Air Chief Marshal Sir Neville McNamara began his term as Chief of Defence Force Staff.

title: General Sir Phillip Bennett became Chief of Defence Force Staff
body: General Sir Phillip Bennett began his term as Chief of Defence Force Staff.

title: General Sir Phillip Bennett became Chief of Defence Force
body: General Sir Phillip Bennett began his term as Chief of Defence Force.

title: General Peter Gration became Chief of Defence Force
body: General Peter Gration began his term as Chief of Defence Force.

title: Admiral Alan Beaumont became Chief of Defence Force
body: Admiral Alan Beaumont began his term as Chief of Defence Force.

title: General John Baker became Chief of Defence Force
body: General John Baker began his term as Chief of Defence Force.

title: Admiral Chris Barrie became Chief of Defence Force
body: Admiral Chris Barrie began his term as Chief of Defence Force.

title: General Peter Cosgrove became Chief of Defence Force
body: General Peter Cosgrove began his term as Chief of Defence Force.
